item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn, Paddy the Pelican is a scary MF. It's amazing to think that some people are exactly that demented and unhinged in real life too. Upvoted with pleasure, and I leave it at +199. Congrats!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Death row inmates don't have families?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This Article made my come up with a head cannon that not all d-class are prisoners, or death row inmates; some are just volunteers that agreed to be part of a test for money like a basic control group.
Reapin' what you've sown.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

While talking with daveyoufool about their headcanon for this piece, they pointed me in the direction of SCP-1722. Given SCP-2835's constant need for criticism, it may not be so unusual for a researcher at the Foundation to suggest 1722's use on the cartoon. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Addendum: Effective 11/19/16, further viewing of SCP-2835 has been suspended indefinitely by Dr. Naismith ( Note: We can't take any more chances. Even if he's bluffing, it's specific bluffing, and the implications are, at best, troubling. - Dr. Naismith ).

To this:

Addendum: Effective 11/19/16, further viewing of SCP-2835 has been suspended indefinitely by Dr. Naismith. Proposals to resume viewing with SCP-1722 present are currently pending review. ( Note: We can't take any more chances. Even if he's bluffing, it's specific bluffing, and the implications are, at best, troubling. - Dr. Naismith ).

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd imagine that Paddy the Pelican is Safe in much the same way that Bobble the Clown is Safe. Not so much that he doesn't try to get out, as much as he can't get out if you don't interact with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beg your pardon, but the "Safe" classification for the object doesn't seem… correct, especially given the addendum at the end. If the researchers are worried that harm could come to an individual, that would indicate to me that it's a Euclid-class item.
It is entirely possible that it's safe if not interacted with, but the researchers clearly do not know and are unwilling to test it, so that says "Euclid" to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aside from insulting the viewer, I don't see how this does anything Bobble (993) doesn't. It doesn't even need containment, if not for the knowledge of people's home addresses, this would just be an anomalous object.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think a mention should be made that the family was checked on and the results. Since it seems the previous iterations actually showed mass murder effects and downvotes due to that it would probably be stating they are safe.

I would however like it to be a single check and note that they are being monitored ending there as a cliff hanger.

The first experiment does not need a results at all as it seemed more like a threat without backing. The second experiment just seems completely unfoundation like to not check post completion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I finally know why I love this SCP so much after having a long thinking session about it in my daydreams.

A long time ago when I didn't have an account here I had an idea of a SCP about a lost episode of Dora The Explorer. The episode will go on forever and Dora can address the viewer. The episode has tons of choices like in original Dora episodes, that will get more complex and hard as time goes on, as well as more deadly for the main character.

Now this idea I don't like as much as I used to, but now I am happy because someone made a cartoon that addresses you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I logged in on mobile to upvote this, as this is one of the best SCPs I've read in recent memory. It just leaves me wanting another test log… Maybe one where the researcher is more cooperative with the SCP? Anyways, it's great. The Sinsekai illustration is actually what led me to this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: just commenting to let you know that Sinsekai20130227 made fan art of this, along with a few others

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels like it's trying to get some kind of reaction from me, but it just isn't working out. Paddy switching from demanding criticism to losing his shit at any negative feedback just doesn't sit well with me, and the instant repetition of "REAP WHAT YOU SOW" really gets on my nerves. I don't like the character, and the article doesn't have any other legs to stand on.
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was essentially my reaction. It's been done before, and better. This version brings nothing new to the table and seems to rely on impotent rage, which doesn't work super-well. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

The random Biblical quote feels like a cheap attempt to make this article sounds deeper, and the final line about testing being suspended just because it can guesses things is so out of character for a foundation researcher. If anything he would've ordered more test ro be done to see the extent of 2835's ability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't see the "Bobble" part in this, yes he is a living cartoon but he isn't really killing people in his cartoon. He just wants people to know what they think of it, but alias since he isn't really good at the cartoons he gets angry when people criticize him.

Much different from bobble in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bobble, but his bark is worse than his bite. No thank you,

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Best tale idea

A whole bunch of media SCPs get crunched together and start talking to eachother.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was an "eh, why not." upvote starting at around the second or third revision, but I still have to say that the latest revision is the strongest one so far. I particularly like the closing statement here, as it strikes a good balance of "possible threat?" to "and then the foundation put the video of the angry pelican back in the lead-lined case and returned it to the dusty shelf across from SCP-993"

Bobble: Told you not to lay it on too thick.
Paddy: Shuddup.
…
Bobble: Manage to get any smokes?
Paddy: Depends. That painting still owe you a favor?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, I'm torn on this one.

I like the idea of this object being a means of the animator to seek validation for his work, and not responding well to the criticism, but that end is just not played well enough to take this beyond simply being a cartoon that threatens people.

I'm going to no vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have initially based my decision to remove the image on the information from its wikipedia page, since it's almost always correct. The wikipedia page, as you said, claimed that the image is copyrighted fair use.

However, after rechecking it now via the US Copyright Office Online Database and the Hirtle chart, it does appear that the show is now in Public Domain. In light of this, I'm reinstating the image. Sorry about the inconvenience.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent. Flows much better tonally now while still maintaining a bit of menace at the end. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Adventures of Paddy the Pelican appear to all have a registered copyright date of 1954 and this wasn't renewed, therefore it should be in the public domain.

ETA: Although Wikipedia is claiming fair use, so maybe I am missing something (or they are being overly cautious).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree, and an edit was made in this regard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dislike the ending note. Yeah, I know that this has gone over a couple more than a couple revisions and pandering to fans might be becoming annoying at this point, but I still feel like the researcher would be more professional within the document.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feels more polished now. I think taking out the anomalous killing was a good idea. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Makes sense

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Viewed this and left it as a novote because of the killing, for the same reasons already described.

Upvoted now. It works much better, especially as in my head the pelican doesn't actually control when the episode ends. So I have a great mental image of him getting really into his ranty threats then just going 'OH SON OF A BITCH' when his D-class target abruptly vanishes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is no less coherent a transition then anything else in a real Paddy Pelican cartoon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it works well enough as a main SCP rather than a -J.
+1

I do feel like it might be lacking some details. The episode always lasts for 30 minutes, right? Does Paddy influence the episodes to make them that long? Or do the credits just start rolling whetever he wants it or not? Will it keep playing if the viewer leaves?

But those are just details. And they might make the SCP longer than neccesary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't mind killing much, but it's really better this way, because it's not that it can't kill people, it's that it's an entity that at least knows everything any viewer does, knows how to threaten people with that information, and we don't actually know if it can act upon it.

And yet it's safe because it's in a reliable bottle. Good stuff.

item.title: Update
item.content:encoded: I've received enough criticisms to the effect of "the fact that Paddy can kill people made me downvote" to seriously consider a change in that regard. Doing so immediately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry I have to downvote echoing the criticisms above. I was really diggin it, especially when the D-Class told him to fuck off. Then shit inexplicably got real (not in a funny or good way) and just OTT.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know I have commented on this a lot but how would the episode go normally? How does one go from eatin the cream to talking about real fathers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it up until the mass murder addenda. That felt very cliche and kind of killed the article for me. It didn't jive at all with the aggressively attention-demanding narcissistic pelican article that preceded it. In addition, the first dialogue bit felt very weak compared to the second one. I would've liked to see more development of the article instead of ending with massive death and destruction. It almost feels like it(the addenda) came out of a completely different SCP. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's fair. My bad

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact anqxyr or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

The image appears to be in Public Domain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey I made a VHS quality version of the photo you used since it comes in a tape. Tell me what you think

PICTURE DELETED

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That settles it. Cause of death changed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmmm… let me think about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Voct's Pelican based death is a pretty good idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, the worst cartoon in the universe is the animated adaptation of Frank Miller's Holy Terror, which nobody remembers thanks to the brave Time Cops who gave their lives to erase it from history.

But yeah, pretty good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but didn't Poe capitalize it, as "Conqueror Worm" ?

And what's the connection between that and dehydration? The Conqueror Worm devoured gorily, which is a sign of death but also a very vivid affirmation of (the Worm's) life.

edit: actually, what if his method of killing was more pelican-based? Say, the D-class's family home was replaced by a crater with pelican feathers in it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a good anomalous media skip, but I feel like it's missing something. I'm not sure what.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was an angry, rambling reference to his method of killing, i.e. he dehydrates his victims and the surrounding area, similar to rapid aging and decay. "Conqueror worm" is being used here as a symbol of decay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, under the "Funny" tab. I'll be damned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: also, why's he referencing Edgar Allan Poe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was the "woah" moment of the scip, I was definitely thrown off by that part and I imagine others were as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I wish he wasn't capable of anomalous murder and was simply an angry and ultimately impotent little cartoon god, I like the dialogue enough to upvote.

item.title: Re: reap whatcha sow
item.content:encoded: Cool I was hoping you would make art of this soon. Pretty cool!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not the English one, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was. Impressively fast, if I've to say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rioghail pretty much said all I wanted to.

item.title: reap whatcha sow
item.content:encoded:
+ A relevant addition to my art page.
- YOU SOW IT YOU REAP IT, BITCH

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, this is on TV tropes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've to admit. I came here running from TV Tropes with intent to downvote since the description there pretty much say "someone ignore the lockbox test and call this thing Keter".

I'm glad that you already fix that and the rest is good enough, so I upvote it instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of an angry animated pelican who hates being stuck in a bad cartoon, and Paddy's characterisation is pretty amusing. I also like the presentation of the link with the WENR-TV incident, because it evokes the sort of things you'd see in discussion of conspiracies or paranormal events quite well.

Unfortunately, making Paddy able to kill people is extremely predictable and ruined the article for me. The dehydration mechanism is seemingly totally arbitrary, and the fact that Paddy can go around killing large numbers of people for very little reason makes this yet another 'super-petty anomalous god-being', which is a story I've seen too many times to interest me. It also undermines Paddy's characterisation as insecure and desperate for human validation because of how bad his cartoon is, because he has superpowers. It'd work better as a story if Paddy was either comically impotent or vindictive but limited in his abilities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made an edit regarding that…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the switches of classes and the finalized containment notes are unnecessary, otherwise a pretty good scip.
